disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden-onset severe constant epigastric pain radiating straight through to the mid-back, present ~6 hours
trigger_question_examples: Where exactly is the pain and does it go anywhere?
When did it start and has it eased at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's right in the middle of my upper belly and it bores straight through to my back. It started suddenly about six hours ago and it hasn't let up at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain worse lying supine, partially relieved by leaning forward (positional epigastric pain)
trigger_question_examples: Is there any position that makes it better or worse?
What happens when you lie flat?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Lying flat on my back is awful, it makes it much worse. Leaning forward takes the edge off a little.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Profound nausea with two episodes of non-bilious, non-bloody vomiting
trigger_question_examples: Have you been sick or felt like vomiting?
Was there any blood in the vomit?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I feel horribly sick and I've thrown up twice. There was no blood in it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known familial dyslipidaemia (high triglycerides/cholesterol) with lipid-lowering therapy ceased ~3 months ago
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any ongoing medical conditions?
Are you on any regular medications, and are you taking them?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've got a family kind of high blood fats. I was on a fibrate and a cholesterol tablet but I stopped them about three months ago because I thought they were giving me side effects.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I didn't think stopping the tablets would matter much.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A large high-fat takeaway meal was eaten earlier the same evening (dietary trigger)
trigger_question_examples: What did you eat before this started?
Was there anything different about your evening?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I had a big greasy takeaway earlier tonight, more than I'd usually eat.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Denies recent alcohol use and denies any known gallstones
trigger_question_examples: Have you been drinking alcohol recently?
Have you ever been told you have gallstones?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I don't really drink and I haven't had any. I've never been told I have gallstones.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Calf cramping on walking that eases with rest (intermittent claudication — premature peripheral arterial disease)
trigger_question_examples: Any problems with your legs when you walk?
Do your calves cramp or ache with activity?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it, my calves cramp up and ache when I walk a bit and it settles when I stop.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Feels feverish and sweaty with the heart pounding
trigger_question_examples: Do you feel hot or sweaty?
Does your heart feel like it's racing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I feel hot and clammy and my heart feels like it's pounding.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lives alone with no one present tonight
trigger_question_examples: Is anyone with you right now?
Who's at home with you tonight?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I'm on my own here tonight.
